text:
September 4th: Part 13 (94⑬, Kugatsu Yokka (Jū San)) is the 114 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

With Gon and Killua restrained, the Phantom Troupe members suddenly realize that Chrollo is missing. Looking again at the knife stuck in the wall, Nobunaga asks Shizuku to hold Gon while Machi checks on Pakunoda, who replies that she’s fine besides a broken left arm and molar. Machi says that she has a few broken ribs, stressing that they underestimated the two boys as she continues to restrain Killua. Pakunoda prepares to tell the others about Gon and Killua's memories, but after removing the note from the knife and reading it, Nobunaga hands it to Pakunoda and says that it's for her.

Pakunoda reads the note which says that Chrollo will die if she speaks of the boys' memories. She then uses her ability on the note and sees Kurapika writing the note and holding a chained Chrollo. The man who was talking to Kurapika wonders what's going on and asks when the power is coming back. Pakunoda realizes that Kurapika was pretending to be the receptionist, who was the one person they weren't watching. She says that Chrollo could have easily dodged the chains in the time it would have taken them to reach him, but the power going out delayed his reaction time by just enough.

She realizes that the two boys knew about the plan, but that it wasn't completely arranged ahead of time since they only decided to go to the Hotel Beitacle recently. She then asks herself why she couldn't tell that the two knew about the chain user the first time she checked them when they both know about him now. She deduces that Kurapika was hiding his ability even from his friends, adding that he must be intelligent and cold-blooded. She understands that the threat to kill Chrollo isn't a bluff and that his colleague named Melody will be able to tell if she reveals the secret to the other Troupe members. She realizes that Kurapika is willing to let his own friends die, but also recognizes that they are valuable as hostages and can be used to get Chrollo back.

As Pakunoda remains deep in thought, Nobunaga snaps her out of it by repeating her name. He tells her not to say anything and takes Killua from Machi so that she can focus on her threads. He is confident that the perpetrators will contact them after leaving a message and two hostages behind. With Gon and Killua both telling themselves that Kurapika successfully carried out the plan, Nobunaga stresses that they should prioritize keeping the two boys as hostages, adding that they need to wait for the other Troupe members to show up.

Pakunoda then has a flashback to when the Troupe was formed in Meteor City. Chrollo tells the six other members that he is the head and they are the legs. He adds that while the legs do what the head says, sometimes they will be more important than him, stressing that someone else will take over if he dies. As Machi whispers to Pakunoda to ask if it’s Chrollo, he continues by saying that while his orders are most important, his life isn't. He finally tells them never to forget that the Spider as a whole needs to survive, not just one individual.

Back in the present, Pakunoda asks herself about the current situation and says that they could easily defeat the chain user if she just told them about his ability and weakness. However, she worries about Chrollo dying for them and says that it will be just like what happened to Uvogin. As she wonders what Chrollo's fortune said, she suddenly remembers her own fortune which mentioned betrayal and the reaper waiting in a cramped room. She wonders if the betrayal refers to talking or staying quiet, also guessing that the boys are the reaper. She remembers Chrollo's words about the survival of the whole and wonders if the cramped room refers to her own mind. She then remembers that since it's still Saturday, that part of the poem won't come into play until the following week.

As Pakunoda wonders which choice she should make and asks herself what Chrollo would do, Machi snaps her out of it and tells her just to stay quiet and not think about it. After the hotel's power comes back on, Nobunaga calls Phinks and the others to tell them to hurry since Chrollo has been taken. Pakunoda knows that Machi is right and tells herself that they need him back even if it means betraying the Spiders.

Elsewhere in the city, Chrollo sits chained in the back seat next to Kurapika. With Melody in the passenger seat, Leorio checks his rearview mirror, but Kurapika assures him that they won't be able to give chase until the other Troupe members join them, since some of them were injured. Chrollo eyes Kurapika, who sternly asks what he's looking at. Chrollo replies that he didn't expect the chain user to be a woman, but Kurapika removes his wig and warns him to watch what he says. Chrollo tells Kurapika that he won't kill him since he left his friends behind, but Kurapika says that he isn't in the mood for his threats. As Leorio tries to calm Kurapika down, Chrollo adds that the event wasn't even mentioned in his fortune, which means that it's completely insignificant. With his eyes widening in anger, Kurapika calls him coward as Melody tries to get his attention and Leorio threatens to kill him if he kills Chrollo.

Characters in Order of Appearance

Trivia

Despite Shalnark not appearing in the flashback scene (or at all during this chapter), he was in fact present at the founding of the Troupe as later revealed in Chapter 397. The spot on the panel in this flashback scene where he would have been was blocked by Chrollo’s speech bubble.
